ncs_standard_definition.description_short: A Heritage Consultant works with government agencies, NGOs, and private companies to preserve and promote India's cultural heritage. They assess, conserve, and manage historical sites, monuments, and artifacts.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Heritage Consultants work on-site to assess the condition of historical sites, monuments, and artifacts. They develop and implement conservation plans, collaborate with stakeholders, and ensure compliance with government regulations and international standards.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: Master's degree in Heritage Conservation, History, Archaeology, or a related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in history, culture, and the arts can pursue a degree in Heritage Studies or a related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue a postgraduate diploma or certification in Heritage Conservation to bridge the gap.

future_builder.description: Heritage Consultants with 2-5 years of experience can advance to senior roles or start their own consulting firms.

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into Heritage Consulting by acquiring relevant skills and experience.

roadmap.title: Foundations in Heritage Studies
resources_keywords: Indian National Trust for Art and Cultural Heritage (INTACH)
